claim: Agent body replaces (not supplements) Claude's generic base system prompt â€” but CLAUDE.md files and project memory load through normal message flow and ARE inherited in all standard subagent invocations. The agent body must supply explicitly: stack versions, pattern examples, structured return formats.
evidence: D1b official docs lines 52, 72, 77 confirm CLAUDE.md loads automatically. V1 C-001 MODIFIED: original claim misrepresented its own source. D6c override gap synthesis independently confirmed. DR1 D1 dispute resolution: threat model is context incompleteness, not security boundary absence.

claim: The description field is the primary routing signal for agent auto-delegation; without <example> blocks, delegation reliability is significantly lower
evidence: CL1 upgraded from MEDIUM: official Claude Code docs explicitly state "Claude uses each subagent description to decide when to delegate"; Anthropic's own pr-review-toolkit plugin agents use <example> blocks inside description field (GitHub confirmed). Two independent Tier-1 sources. D1b + D6c also confirm. G3: pure LLM reasoning mechanism confirmed — description quality directly affects routing fidelity.

claim: Agent name case-insensitivity is UNCONFIRMED — official docs mandate lowercase-only names; no official source confirms case-insensitive runtime resolution
evidence: CL1 downgraded from MEDIUM: official docs specify name field as "lowercase letters and hyphens" hard constraint. No official source confirms case-insensitive runtime matching. GitHub Issue #9206 (closed) shows names are matched strictly. GV2 confirmed explore.md has name: explore (lowercase); CLAUDE.md uses Explore (capital) for display only. Design agents as lowercase; update CLAUDE.md references to match canonical names.

claim: The official agent frontmatter schema has 16 fields: name, description, tools, disallowedTools, model, permissionMode, maxTurns, skills, mcpServers, hooks, memory, background, effort, isolation, initialPrompt, color
evidence: D1b confirmed from official plugin-dev SKILL.md. V1 C-004 MODIFIED: D1b header says '17 fields' but the table contains exactly 16 entries â€” off-by-one error in D1b source. Counting the list yields 16. Original claim propagated the off-by-one.

claim: Plugin agents cannot use hooks, mcpServers, or permissionMode frontmatter fields
evidence: D1b confirmed from official plugin-dev docs; confirmed by D3b global agent inventory

claim: The 500-2000 token sweet spot for agent bodies is empirically supported; agents below ~500 tokens lack specificity, above ~2000 tokens push instructions out of attention window; current extremes: fullstack-developer (1281 lines), security-engineer (985 lines)
evidence: V1 C-006 VERIFIED (partially): line counts confirmed from filesystem. fullstack-developer=1281L, security-engineer=985L match exactly. The 500-2000 token empirical claim is from external best-practices (UNVERIFIABLE against filesystem).

claim: The ecosystem mean agent quality score is 54/100 (F grade) post-session improvement as of the one audit run on 2026-03-17. Initial mean was 51/100. All 36 agents were scored. 18 improvement ACTIONS were deferred â€” not 18 unscored agents.
evidence: V1 C-007 MODIFIED: audit-agent-quality-history.jsonl confirms agents_audited=36 (all scored), decisions.skip=18 = improvement actions deferred. post_improvement_mean=54. DR1 D9: reframe from crisis to calibration signal.

claim: Five Tier-A agents (score 5/5): code-reviewer, explore, frontend-developer, plan, security-auditor; these are the reference-quality implementations
evidence: V1 C-008 VERIFIED: D3a explicitly lists these 5; filesystem confirms all five exist at substantive sizes (code-reviewer 259L, explore 194L, frontend-developer 243L, plan 209L, security-auditor 534L)

claim: Automated quality enforcement covers 0 of 16 frontmatter fields; check-agent-compliance.js (196 lines) validates agent invocation, not agent quality
evidence: V1 C-009 MODIFIED: script is 196 lines (not 197 as originally claimed). Core claim VERIFIED: no grep for frontmatter, quality, score, or any of the 16 fields found in the script.

claim: check-agent-compliance.test.ts re-implements trigger-pattern logic that does not match check-agent-compliance.js actual behavior, providing false confidence in the compliance system
evidence: V1 C-010 VERIFIED: test implements AGENT_TRIGGERS text-prompt matching; production script implements file-change pattern matching. Completely different behavioral models â€” tests are useless for validating the production script.

claim: Nine local agents are stubs with no meaningful content and should be removed after a cross-reference audit: error-detective, devops-troubleshooter, deployment-engineer, penetration-tester plus 5 others; use 60-day redirect stubs for any with live references
evidence: V1 C-011 VERIFIED: filesystem confirms exactly 9 agents under 50 lines. Manual reads confirm boilerplate. DR1 D6: deletion requires cross-reference audit first; redirect stubs for 60 days where references found.

claim: Consolidation plan: 26 local agents â†’ 17 (35% reduction); action table: REMOVE 9, ELEVATE 3, REPLACE 1, MODIFY 1, KEEP 8, DEFER 2 (active defers)
evidence: V1 C-012 MODIFIED: D7c confirms REMOVE 9, ELEVATE 3, REPLACE 1, MODIFY 1, KEEP 8. DEFER count is 2 active defers (ui-ux-designer + prompt-engineer); backend-architect moved to REMOVE.

claim: Global agents at runtime (~/.claude/agents/) have no model field â€” the sync gap between project-tracked (.claude/agents/global/ with model: sonnet) and runtime paths is a critical infrastructure issue affecting all 12+ GSD agents
evidence: V1 C-013 VERIFIED: grep ~/.claude/agents/*.md returns zero model fields. .claude/agents/global/*.md all have model: sonnet. V2 serendipity: all 12 runtime GSD agents lack model fields (broader than originally characterized).

claim: Six system/plugin agents need project-level overrides: general-purpose (P1), silent-failure-hunter (P1 — wrong logger), pr-test-analyzer (P1 — Jest vs Vitest), code-simplifier (P2), type-design-analyzer (P2). Note: pr-review-toolkit contains 6 agents (not 5) — comment-analyzer is the 6th.
evidence: CL1: all 5 confirmed to exist in official anthropics/claude-code repo (pr-review-toolkit plugin). CL1 serendipity: 6th agent comment-analyzer.md also present. CLAUDE.md is NOT loaded into plugin agents (official docs confirmed). GV2: code-simplifier listed as standalone plugin in REQUIRED_PLUGINS.md — count relationship unclear (MEDIUM confidence). Threat: specificity gap not boundary absence.

claim: The general-purpose project override is the highest-leverage specificity injection action: 13+ invocations across doc-optimizer, audit-*, convergence-loop, pre-commit-fixer lack explicit stack versions, pattern examples, structured returns. CLAUDE.md security rules ARE inherited. Hooks (not the override) handle structural enforcement.
evidence: V1 C-015 VERIFIED: D6b confirms 13+ invocations. DR1 D1+D10: reframed from 'no security boundaries' to 'lacks specificity'. Override and hooks are complementary layers.

claim: Phase 2.5 verification has no prompt template in REFERENCE.md â€” the most critical unresolved pipeline gap; SKILL.md describes it in one sentence
evidence: V1 C-016 VERIFIED: exhaustive grep of REFERENCE.md v1.8 (1405 lines) finds no Phase 2.5 template. REFERENCE.md v1.4+ added templates for other phases (3.5, 3.95, 3.96, 3.97) but NOT 2.5.

claim: Six pipeline roles exist; only searcher (385 lines) and synthesizer (343 lines) have reference-quality custom definitions. REFERENCE.md v1.8 template sizes: contrarian ~26L, OTB ~27L, dispute ~16L code block, gap-pursuer ~37L, final-re-synthesis ~35L. Phase 2.5: no template.
evidence: V1 C-017 MODIFIED + V2 Serendipity: original template sizes (17L, 15L, 29L, 29L) reflect pre-v1.4 REFERENCE.md. REFERENCE.md updated to v1.7+v1.8 on 2026-03-29 expanding all templates. Current sizes measured from v1.8.

claim: Six new custom agent definitions close the deep-research pipeline (closed set): deep-research-verifier (P1, pilot first), contrarian-challenger (P1), otb-challenger (P1), dispute-resolver (P2), deep-research-gap-pursuer (P2), deep-research-final-synthesizer (P2)
evidence: DR1 D2: custom agents justified by structural enforcement requirements (structured returns, artifact discipline, profile-switching). This is a closed set â€” no further pipeline agents needed. D5: hard ceiling on general-duty expansion.

claim: The deep-research-verifier should be unified: one definition covering Phase 2.5 (codebase/VERIFIED-REFUTED) and Phase 3.96 (consistency/CONTRADICTION-CONSISTENT), with scope mode passed at spawn time
evidence: D9b Finding 4 filesystem analysis; V1 C-019 UNVERIFIABLE (design recommendation for non-existent agent). Direction remains sound per D9b structural analysis.

claim: The deep-research-final-synthesizer must cover both Phase 3.9 (>20% claim change) and Phase 3.97 (after gap pursuit); two invocations with general-purpose synthesizer would cause the second to undo Phase 3.9 corrections
evidence: D9b serendipity section; V1 C-020 UNVERIFIABLE (design risk). REFERENCE.md Section 21.2 confirms Phase 3.9 triggers on >20% changed. Architectural hazard is plausible and confirmed by DR1.

claim: Four-verdict taxonomy is the industry standard for verification: VERIFIED, REFUTED, UNVERIFIABLE, CONFLICTED; CONFLICTED is the handoff point to dispute resolution
evidence: D9a convergence across OpenFactCheck, RefChecker, AAR framework, Step-DeepResearch. V1 C-021 UNVERIFIABLE against filesystem â€” external academic/industry claim.

claim: FIRE architecture reduces verification cost 7.6x (LLM API cost) and 16.5x (search API cost) — these are two separate cost axes, not a single range; savings apply to external fact-checking context
evidence: CL1 upgraded from MEDIUM: confirmed in ACL Anthology (aclanthology.org/2025.findings-naacl.158/). 7.6x = LLM inference cost reduction; 16.5x = search/retrieval cost reduction. Two separate metrics, not a range. Codebase claim verification has different cost structure — filesystem reads are the verification mechanism, not LLM API calls. Codebase applicability remains LOW-MEDIUM.

claim: Steel-man before attack is the foundational adversarial agent pattern; pre-mortem framing ('assume this research is wrong in 6 months â€” why?') produces higher-quality challenges than direct attack
evidence: D8a from multiple production adversarial agent implementations; V1 C-023 UNVERIFIABLE against filesystem. Direction validated by CH1-CH4 challenge process itself.

claim: Free-MAD shows 13-16% quality improvement from consensus-free debate; adversarial agents should maintain their challenges even when the synthesizer has high confidence
evidence: D8a from arXiv:2509.xxxxx Free-MAD paper. V1 C-024 UNVERIFIABLE. CH4 BS1: homogeneous model research â€” the quantified improvement figure may reflect model-specific recall. Downgraded from HIGH.

claim: iMAD selective triggering cuts multi-agent debate cost 57-92%: 57-70% vs MAD baseline (dataset-specific), up to 92% vs GroupDebate baseline; applicability as heuristic for prompt-only pipelines not independently validated
evidence: CL1 upgraded from MEDIUM with precision note: paper arXiv:2511.11306 confirmed. Range vs MAD: 57% (ScienceQA) to 70% (MMLU, GSM8K); 92% vs GroupDebate only. The 68% lower bound in prior claim was accurate only for MEDQA vs MAD. Full dataset range is 57-70% (vs MAD), up to 92% (vs GroupDebate). RL-trained systems; prompt-only pipeline applicability not validated (direction sound, magnitude uncertain).

claim: DRAGged five-type conflict taxonomy: No Conflict, Complementary, Conflicting Opinions, Freshness, Misinformation; classifying before resolving improves resolution quality in benchmark evaluations
evidence: D9a Finding 7 from DRAGged paper (arXiv:2506.08500). V1 C-026 UNVERIFIABLE. CH4 BS1: quantified improvement (24 points) from external benchmark â€” codebase applicability not separately validated. Downgraded from HIGH.

Model assignments confirmed exactly. Note: plan built-in uses "inherits from main conversation" (not Haiku).

claim: The explore system agent has a quality score of 82/100 and plan has 79/100 per D6a analysis; both lack <example> blocks which is their single highest-leverage improvement
evidence: V2 C-079 VERIFIED: D6a-system-overrides-current.md line 26 states 82/100 for explore, line 89 states 79/100 for plan.

claim: data/ecosystem-v2/invocations.jsonl covers only 2 agent invocations (of 31 entries); agent-invocations.jsonl has 139 entries (was 135 at research time â€” 4 added in same session)
evidence: V2 C-080 MODIFIED: ecosystem file has 31 entries with 2 agent-type entries (confirmed). agent-invocations.jsonl is 139 lines (was 135 at D12b write time; 4 added in same session). Core finding (unreliable Category 7 scoring) unchanged.

claim: Anthropic's Swiss Cheese evaluation model uses three grader types: code-based (fast, deterministic), model-based (LLM-as-judge with rubrics), human (gold standard); no single layer catches everything
evidence: D12a Finding 2 from anthropic.com/engineering/demystifying-evals. V2 C-081 UNVERIFIABLE against filesystem.

claim: No golden test cases exist for any agent; no tests/agents/ directory exists
evidence: V2 C-082 VERIFIED: find tests returns only hook/script tests. No tests/agents/ directory. The check-agent-compliance test tests script logic, not agent content quality.

claim: Step-DeepResearch's verification sub-pipeline uses 4 specialized roles: Extract, Plan, Verify, Replan â€” treating verification as a planning problem with dependency graphs between claims
evidence: D9a Finding 4 from arXiv:2512.20491. V2 C-083 UNVERIFIABLE against filesystem.

claim: Cap gap pursuit at 2-3 rounds max; current 1-round limit (SKILL.md Critical Rule 9) is conservative but defensible; empirical standard of 9-12 is from RL-trained systems not comparable to prompt-only pipelines
evidence: V2 C-084 VERIFIED (architectural decision documented): SKILL.md line 258 confirms Critical Rule 9 'One round only'. D9a Finding 8 from AgentCPM gives 9-12 figure for RL systems. 2-3 rounds is extrapolated estimate.

claim: The audit skill evaluates 13 categories; audit-agent-quality has run once (2026-03-17); 12 days since last run; 3 new unscored agents
evidence: V2 C-085 VERIFIED: audit-agent-quality-history.jsonl has 1 entry dated 2026-03-17 with categories=13. Current count=39 vs audit count=36. Today=2026-03-29 = 12 days.

claim: PR review team opportunity evaluated and RECOMMEND AGAINST: the existing audit-review-team covers high-security-sensitive PRs; a dedicated PR review team would duplicate infrastructure without value for a solo developer
evidence: V2 C-086 REFUTED original 'DEFERRED' characterization: D11b-teams-internal.md line 379 states 'RECOMMEND AGAINST a PR review team for standard PR reviews'. The word 'DEFERRED' does not appear in D11b in this context.

claim: Agent auto-delegation reliability is uncertain in practice; HN discussion surfaced contradiction between docs promise (auto-delegates when description matches) and practitioner experience (Claude often completes task itself)
evidence: D10b contradiction section. V2 C-087 UNVERIFIABLE against filesystem. Unresolved.

claim: GoAgent (March 2026) uses graph diffusion models to auto-generate sparse communication topologies at $5.60 vs $43.70 for dense mesh â€” within 1-2 years manual team design may be obsolete
evidence: D11a serendipity from arXiv:2603.19677. V2 C-088 UNVERIFIABLE. Speculative extrapolation to SoNash.

claim: Prevent redundant revalidation: track which claims have been verified and do not re-verify in downstream stages; a substantial fraction of verification tokens is consumed by redundant revalidation
evidence: D9a Finding 11 from arXiv:2603.22651. V2 C-089 UNVERIFIABLE against filesystem.

claim: Evidence-weight arbitration (not majority voting) is preferred for knowledge-domain disputes; majority voting follows the herd into dominant-but-wrong consensus
evidence: D9a Finding 6 â€” ACL 2025 paper vs AgentAuditor finding. V2 C-090 UNVERIFIABLE. Evidence-weight preferred when adversarial challenges are possible (validated by DR1 dispute resolution process).

claim: color field valid values are: blue, cyan, green, yellow, magenta, red; agents with non-standard values: deep-research-synthesizer (purple) and gsd-debugger (orange)
evidence: V2 C-091 REFUTED original agent identification: dependency-manager and documentation-expert have NO color field. Direct filesystem reads + git history confirmed. Non-standard colors are in deep-research-synthesizer (color: purple) and gsd-debugger (color: orange).

claim: The agent standard template has 11 sections: frontmatter, role, philosophy, upstream_input, execution_flow, structured_returns, success_criteria, and supporting sections
evidence: D1b confirmed 6-section standard template; D9b Finding 9 confirmed 11-section pattern. V2 C-092 UNVERIFIABLE â€” 11-section 'standard' is not a single canonical file.

claim: The upstream_input section is more critical for pipeline agents than for searcher/synthesizer because they receive structured orchestrator-assembled payloads rather than natural-language sub-questions
evidence: D9b Finding 9. V2 C-093 UNVERIFIABLE â€” qualitative architectural judgment.

evidence: V2 C-094 VERIFIED: research-plan-team.md line 60 confirmed 'L or XL'. deep-research SKILL.md lines 77/166/181/198 use L1/L2/L3/L4. Two incompatible systems confirmed by direct filesystem read.

claim: AGENT_ORCHESTRATION.md needs 2 updates as a result of the consolidation program: (1) Architecture grouping table: replace backend-architect with nextjs-architecture-expert/database-architect; (2) capacity table: remove backend-architect row
evidence: V2 C-095 VERIFIED: D7c-consolidation-synthesis.md Section 'AGENT_ORCHESTRATION.md Update Required' line 354 explicitly states 2 targeted updates.

claim: Optimal model selection for pipeline agents: verification and gap-verification = Sonnet; dispute = Sonnet unless >10 disputes; gap pursuit = Sonnet; final re-synthesis = Sonnet; security-auditor migrates to effort: max (= Opus 4.6) at next update
evidence: V2 C-096 VERIFIED: D9b Finding 10 model complexity table confirmed. deep-research-searcher.md and deep-research-synthesizer.md both use model: sonnet confirmed. DR1 D8: security-auditor should migrate from model: opus to effort: max.

claim: All pipeline agents must follow the established 11-section custom agent pattern; the upstream_input section must have explicit schema for structured orchestrator payloads; use skills: [sonash-context] for context injection
evidence: D9b Finding 9 pattern compliance requirement. DR1 D3: sonash-context skill as shared context mechanism for all new pipeline agents.

claim: All 13 global agents in .claude/agents/global/ have model: sonnet. Runtime path ~/.claude/agents/ (12 GSD agents) has NO model field. Project-tracked and runtime global paths are out of sync.
evidence: V2 C-098 MODIFIED: all 13 tracked global agents confirmed model: sonnet. But 12 runtime GSD agents (~/.claude/agents/) have no model field. 'GSD agents' label is imprecise â€” 13 includes 11 gsd-* + 2 deep-research agents.

claim: Optional next-cycle additions: firebase-specialist (P3, after manual Firebase skills validation), refactoring-specialist (P3, creation gate), e2e-testing-specialist (P3, creation gate)
evidence: V2 C-099 VERIFIED: none exist in roster. DR1 D5: all three require creation gate. Firebase-specialist additionally requires manual validation of 3 Firebase skills before agent creation.

claim: The deep-plan planning verification step (Phase 5) uses general-purpose inline with no domain context; a deep-plan-verifier (P3) would check alignment with ROADMAP.md, SESSION_CONTEXT.md, and SWS gate
evidence: D10c Additional Gap A analysis. V2 C-100 UNVERIFIABLE â€” design gap assessment.

claim: P0 Infrastructure steps required before any agent creation: (1) define success metrics (target mean â‰¥75/100), (2) create sonash-context shared skill, (3) verify skills: injection position, (4) fix compliance test, (5) cross-reference audit for 9 removal candidates, (6) extend hooks for structural enforcement
evidence: DR1 D4+D6+D10: infrastructure prerequisites from dispute resolution. P0.5 hook extension: PostToolUse Write hook for direct Firestore import detection covers all 39 agents simultaneously at zero context window cost.

claim: Hard 30-agent ceiling: 17 local (post-consolidation) + 13 global (pipeline agents fit within global slots). Any new agent beyond the 6 pipeline set requires removing one existing agent first (one-in-one-out rule).
evidence: DR1 D7: solo developer feasibility constraint. Current 39 - 9 removed = 30. 6 pipeline agents add to global but may replace roles. One-in-one-out prevents ecosystem drift.

claim: Auto-delegation is pure LLM reasoning — no embeddings, classifiers, or regex matching; all agent names+descriptions are formatted into the Agent tool's prompt and Claude decides during its forward pass
evidence: G3: confirmed from multiple sources including system prompt reverse-engineering and official Claude Code docs. LLM reasoning explains both reliability and failure modes. G3-V2 (GV1): CLAUDE.md Section 7 confirmed as REQUIRED trigger table with prescriptive language.

claim: Three agents have 1-2 character descriptions providing zero routing signal: dependency-manager (1 char), deep-research-searcher (2 chars), deep-research-synthesizer (2 chars); total description budget is 6,725/15,000 chars (45%)
evidence: GV1 G3-V3 MODIFIED: character count confirmed by direct filesystem scan. 45% budget used — description crowding is NOT currently acute. The 3 near-empty descriptions are routing failures. deep-research-* are likely intentional (programmatic spawn); dependency-manager 1 char is likely a bug.

claim: Firebase official agent skills (11 skills): no Cloud Functions skill, no App Check skill, no httpsCallable coverage — all 5 SoNash-critical security patterns are absent from official skills
evidence: G2: direct content inspection of firebase-basics and firebase-firestore-standard SKILL.md files; search across ecosystem. 11 skills confirmed (not 13 as originally stated). Firebase blog acknowledges more skills coming. GV1 G2-V1: httpsCallable confirmed in 12 SoNash files; GV1 G2-V3: Zod confirmed in Cloud Functions.

claim: App Check is architecturally implemented but currently DISABLED (requireAppCheck: false) across ALL Cloud Functions — doc-vs-runtime gap, untracked in MASTER_DEBT
evidence: GV1 G2-V2 MODIFIED: requireAppCheck: false with 'TEMPORARILY DISABLED - waiting for throttle to clear' comment in saveDailyLog, saveJournalEntry, softDeleteJournalEntry, saveInventoryEntry; migrateAnonymousUserData has App Check block commented out entirely. CLAUDE.md Section 2 states 'App Check Required' — current runtime state contradicts this. No issue tracker reference.

claim: Firebase MCP server provides live Firestore queries, Auth user lookup, Functions log retrieval — capabilities SoNash agents currently lack; not configured in settings.json
evidence: G2 Finding 5; GV1 G2-V4 VERIFIED: no mcpServers key in settings.json, no Firebase in enabledPlugins. MCP server is bundled with claude.com/plugins/firebase. Does not expose App Check or httpsCallable construction, but provides operational tooling. Evaluate as P0 infrastructure step.

claim: Per-agent team initialization costs 20,000-85,000 tokens; idle notification flood is lead-side (processing notifications), not teammate-side (generating output)
evidence: G4 Q2: initialization breakdown from aicosts.ai practitioner estimates. Idle notification correction: prior framing was wrong — teammates don't cost tokens while waiting, the lead's context costs tokens processing the notifications. GV2 G4-1: team configs confirmed accurate including idle flood documentation.

claim: TeammateIdle hook is absent from SoNash settings.json — documented only as a gap in research files; adding it to audit-review-team would provide quality gate enforcement
evidence: GV2 G4-2 CONFIRMED ABSENT: TeammateIdle not in settings.json. Found only in worktree research docs as a gap recommendation. G4: official Claude Code docs document TeammateIdle hook with exit code 2 for quality gate enforcement. Prevents idle notification flood and redirects teammates to pending tasks.

claim: Anthropic validated Agent Teams at scale: 16 sequential team runs across ~2,000 sessions rewrote a C compiler in Rust producing ~100K lines of code at ~$20,000 (2B input tokens, 140M output tokens)
evidence: G4 Serendipity: from Anthropic engineering blog. '16 agent teams' = 16 sequential runs (one-team-per-session limit still in effect). Validates that teams can work at scale for complex engineering tasks with well-defined separation of concerns.

claim: CVE-2025-59536 (March 2026, RCE via Claude Code project files): zero SoNash agents use bypassPermissions or dontAsk — attack surface reduced but all 39 agent files in version control remain a supply chain risk
evidence: GV1 G3-V1 VERIFIED: grep for permissionMode, bypassPermissions, dontAsk across all 39 agent files returns zero matches. G3 Serendipity: CVE documented by Check Point Research. Reduced attack surface but not eliminated — injection via agent file content still possible regardless of permissionMode.
